frontmatter.title: University of California Open Summit (UC Open) 2026
frontmatter.description: The Second Annual UC Open celebrating open source throughout the UC system: 22 & 23 April 2026 – UC Berkeley

children.value: As open source reshapes research, infrastructure, and how academia connects with industry, the UC Open Source Summit 2026 brings together two days of collaboration and partnership. Researchers, developers, students, and industry leaders from across the University of California system will explore what’s next—from AI’s impact on academia to building sustainable research infrastructure.

Discover emerging UC projects before they reach the mainstream, build partnerships on production-ready technologies, and connect with the next generation of contributors. Whether you’re looking for research partners, hiring talent, solving technical problems, or contributing expertise back to the academic open source community, UC Open 2026 connects university innovation with real-world application.
This isn’t just another conference. UC Open is:
: we build in breaks long enough to recharge your attention span and have real conversations.

: our deliberately limited capacity means you’ll leave with real relationships, not just business cards. Every conversation matters when you’re part of a curated community rather than a sea of strangers.
: this event is for humans, not sales pitches. Whether you’re a researcher, maintainer, contributor, or simply curious about open source innovation, you’ll find your people here.
Thanks to the generosity of our sponsors, UC Open is

for all participants! We will provide breakfast and lunch on both days, as well as an evening reception on Wednesday. When you register you will be asked to provide any dietary restrictions or concerns.

Chaired by Karla Padilla (UCSD). Panelists: Heather Meeker (Chinstrap Community), Mike Cohen (UC Berkeley), Dirk Riehle (FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg), Joel Kehle (UCLA)

Panelists: Fernando Perez (UC Berkeley), Sahiba Chopra (UC Berkeley), Marit MacArthur (UC Davis)
Hidden in Plain Sight: Discovering the University of California Open Source Landscape

An Open-Source Ecosystem for Building Weather Driven Agricultural Decision Tools

Support open source collaboration and connect with top academic and industry talent by sponsoring UC Open 2026. Sponsorship provides visibility, networking, and alignment with a community driving innovation for public benefit.
: Host a workshop session + premium event visibility
: Sponsor a presentation session + strong branding
: Support meals, coffee breaks, and receptions
All sponsors are prominently recognized on signage, the website, and promotional materials. Let’s build the future of open source together—
to explore how sponsorship can align with your organization’s goals.
The Brower Center, 2150 Allston Way, Berkeley, CA 94704

Accessibility: the Brower Center has steps and a ramp to the front entrance, and an elevator for those who cannot take the stairs. Wheelchair seating is available in the Goldman Theater auditorium—up to three wheelchairs can be accommodated with advance notice. Every bathroom has an accessible stall. Please
if you have accessibility needs so we can make arrangements.
The Brower Center is in downtown Berkeley, steps from the
Weekday parking is available in the Oxford Garage directly below the Brower Center (enter on Kittredge St). The garage is open 7 AM–midnight with a live attendant 10 AM–7 PM on weekdays. See

Secure bike racks are available in the Oxford Garage (enter via Allston), staffed 10 AM–7 PM on weekdays. Free bike valet is also available at the Bike Station at 2023 Center Street (7 AM–7 PM weekdays), a few blocks away.
The following hotels are within walking distance of the Brower Center:
DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel Berkeley Marina
, including a YMCA (4-minute walk) and UC Berkeley lodging options.
Preferred rates may be available for UC-affiliated attendees via
Oakland International Airport (OAK)—about 12 miles, BART accessible
San Francisco International Airport (SFO)—about 25 miles, BART accessible
A limited number of travel grants are available for UC affiliates attending UC Open 2026.
